commit_msg: feat(ui): add keyboard acceleration and focus management
author: Omkar
title: CASE FILE — Fingers Made Faster
subtitle: Keyboard acceleration: the difference between thinking about escape and actually running.
when_where: 🕑 2:03 PM, same branch, same afternoon. The UI was already dangerous, but now Omkar gave it *velocity*. ⌨️ Keyboard shortcuts, focus management—tools for the impatient, the panicked, the man who knew he didn't have much time left before someone noticed.
who_whom: 🔥 The user and the interface were no longer separate. Omkar had fused them—every keystroke now carried momentum, every focus shift was a decision made before the brain could second-guess it. 🎯 The app didn't just respond to input anymore; it anticipated it, pulled the user forward faster than they meant to go.
what_why: ⚙️ He added keyboard acceleration because slowness was his enemy. Every millisecond of lag was a chance for hesitation, for doubt, for someone to ask 'why are you running this so fast?' 🧠 Focus management meant the app would *decide* where your attention went next, not you. 💀 He wasn't building a feature; he was building a escape velocity. The faster the interface, the less time you have to think about what you're actually doing.
verdict: He didn't accelerate the keyboard; he accelerated the forgetting.
